frame_analyses.description: Setup of the laparoscopic trainer is visible with suture thread placed on the Penrose drain.
Right instrument approaches to load the needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Careful approach for needle loading observed.

frame_analyses.description: Needle passed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears close to mark.

frame_analyses.description: Needle passed through the second mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precision maintained, suture pass close to second mark noted.

frame_analyses.description: Initiation of the surgeon's knot as the first throw with a C-shaped knot formation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct interpretation of the surgeon's knot with double throw.

frame_analyses.description: Completion of double throw for the first knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Strong control maintained during the formation of the double throw.

frame_analyses.description: Switch to the left hand and make the second single throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch noted, and precision maintained for throw.

frame_analyses.description: Switch to right hand and make the third single throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Another successful hand switch for final throw, good tension applied.

frame_analyses.description: Both ends of the suture being cut inside the trainer.

knot_assessments.notes: Surgeon's knot done with double throw, appears secure.
Single throw done with hand switch, secure.
Single throw with another hand switch, secure.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap or damage, complete closure.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated proficient needle handling and knot tying. Suture placement was precise with minimal deviation. Hand switching between throws was executed well.
improvement_suggestions: Practice suture placement to reduce deviation.
Refine hand switching technique for smoother transitions.
